mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held near the lower bound, the discount rate on equities declines and duration-sensitive large-cap earnings are valued more highly, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a declining or very low FEDFUNDS setting, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration above prior trend raises expected policy tightening and compresses equity multiples, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal loses explanatory power once FEDFUNDS is already rising.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates deteriorating household income and weaker future consumption, so equity prices should fall over the following quarter as analysts mark down earnings.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-shock episodes are followed by nonnegative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS is simultaneously improving.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both improve together, it signals broad domestic demand momentum in consumption and housing, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous upswings in retail sales and housing starts do not produce positive 20-day returns more often than baseline, or if either series alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO after a prior slowdown reflects re-acceleration in real activity and manufacturing utilization, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next week through earnings revision momentum.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns up after at least 2 consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if industrial production rebounds are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect is absent after controlling for the broader market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 growth combined with flat retail sales signals weakening final demand and lower future corporate revenue, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth falls below 1.0% while RSAFS growth is negative
failure_condition: Reject if low-growth, weak-sales episodes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in most cases, or if the joint condition adds no predictive value beyond GDPC1 alone.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS continues to rise while UNRATE stays flat or declines, labor income is broadening without recessionary stress, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE does not rise and PAYEMS increases over 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if payroll growth with stable or falling unemployment fails to produce positive 20-day returns at least 55% of the time, or if the relationship flips negative during nonrecession periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST after a prior high level indicates tightening housing supply and a slowdown in interest-sensitive real activity, which should weaken equity returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 3-month average while FEDFUNDS is above 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start declines under a positive policy-rate backdrop do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than chance, or if the sign is reversed when FEDFUNDS is elevated.
